text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division 

Clinic Date: 21 September 2025 

Dr Fiona  
Centre for Epilepsy  
Queen Square, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Fiona,

Susan Miller, DOB: 14-05-1986, Hospital No: P948572 NHS No. 7623549812  
12 Baker Street, London, NW1 6XE  

Epilepsy – Focal epilepsy

Medication: Lamotrigine restarted earlier this year. Currently on lamotrigine dispersible 50 mg in the morning and 100 mg at night (patient-reported adherence good; no missed doses in the last month). No other anti-seizure medicines.

Bloods: Routine lamotrigine level pending from last week (requested to establish trough level given recent increase). U&Es and LFTs reportedly within normal ranges from GP testing in August.

History since last review: Susan Miller works in a care setting with irregular breaks and frequent patient transfers. She reports cumulative fatigue and perceived worsening of her epilepsy associated with shift patterns and disrupted sleep. Importantly, seizure freedom continues was the case until late July; however, over the past six weeks she now describes focal aware events progressing on occasion to impaired awareness. She experiences brief right temporal tingling and rising epigastric discomfort, followed by staring and speech arrest lasting 1–2 minutes. These have become frequent, with seizures every other day. There have been no witnessed generalised tonic–clonic seizures.

Seizure description (patient-reported):
- Focal aware onset with right-sided tingling and an epigastric rising sensation.
- Evolution to impaired awareness with behavioural arrest and non-responsiveness for up to 2 minutes; post-event fatigue for about 30 minutes.
- No tongue biting, no incontinence, occasional headache post-event.

Seizure frequency: Deterioration from previous stability; seizures every other day over the last six weeks.

Triggers and context: Sleep disruption due to shift work and manual handling demands at work seem contributory; she associates clusters with sequences of late-to-early shifts and missed meal breaks. She has started keeping a diary; entries correlate with fatigue days. No recent intercurrent illness, alcohol use minimal, no new medications apart from a short course of ibuprofen.

Safety and impact: Two near-miss incidents at work during patient transfers due to sudden impaired awareness. She has stopped driving and has informed DVLA. She is requesting occupational health review regarding duties and breaks.

Plan discussed today (patient-reported preferences and observations):
- Continue current lamotrigine dosing pending serum level result and consultant review, as she prefers not to change dose immediately before rota adjustments. 
- Arrange urgent epilepsy nurse follow-up and video-EEG consideration if events persist at current rate.
- Provide written seizure first-aid advice for her workplace and encourage structured breaks and sleep hygiene.

I will update you once the lamotrigine level is back and after consultant case review. In summary, this is focal epilepsy with a clear deterioration from prior seizure freedom to seizures every other day, associated with occupational fatigue and irregular hours.

Yours sincerely

entities.text: seizures every other day over the last six weeks

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter clearly states the current frequency: "seizures every other day over the last six weeks" and "These have become frequent, with seizures every other day." "Every other day" equates to 1 seizure per 2 days. No other higher frequency is provided. Not seizure free (recent deterioration within 6 weeks), so we quantify current frequency.
seizure_frequency_number: seizures every other day over the last six weeks
